# Confidential Computing Deep Dive

> A comprehensive guide to Confidential Computing, TEEs, attestation,
> Confidential VMs, and CNCF Confidential Containers (CoCo).
> Author: Pradipta Banerjee, Project Maintainer — Confidential Containers

## Chapters

- [Introduction](https://confidential-devhub.github.io/cc-book/chapters/intro.md): This book is a comprehensive deep dive into Confidential Computing. It covers the theory, architecture, components, and use cases to understand and work with confidential computing.
- [What is Confidential Computing?](https://confidential-devhub.github.io/cc-book/chapters/overview.md): Modern data security is often described using three pillars:
- [Use Cases](https://confidential-devhub.github.io/cc-book/chapters/use-cases.md): Here are the primary use cases of confidential computing.
- [Building Blocks](https://confidential-devhub.github.io/cc-book/chapters/building-blocks.md): This section will take you through some of the foundational elements of confidential computing.
- [Remote Attestation](https://confidential-devhub.github.io/cc-book/chapters/remote-attestation.md): Remote attestation is the mechanism by which a TEE proves to a remote party that it is genuine, unmodified, and running the expected software. Without attestation, Confidential Computing would just be
- [The Trust Boundary Problem](https://confidential-devhub.github.io/cc-book/chapters/trust-boundary.md): With the building blocks in place — Root of Trust, measured boot, and TEEs — we can now ask the critical question: do they actually protect tenants from the infrastructure they run on?
- [Three Pillars of Confidential Computing](https://confidential-devhub.github.io/cc-book/chapters/three-pillars.md): Confidential Computing in cloud-native environments is organized into three deployment tiers, each building on the previous one.
- [Confidential Virtual Machine (CVM)](https://confidential-devhub.github.io/cc-book/chapters/confidential-vms.md): A Confidential Virtual Machine (CVM) is a virtual machine that runs inside a hardware Trusted Execution Environment (TEE), protecting the guest's memory and execution from inspection or tampering by t
- [Confidential Containers (CoCo)](https://confidential-devhub.github.io/cc-book/chapters/confidential-containers.md): Confidential Containers — a generic term for containers deployed inside Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs).
- [Trustee](https://confidential-devhub.github.io/cc-book/chapters/trustee.md): [Trustee](https://github.com/confidential-containers/trustee) is the remote attestation and secret brokering infrastructure for CNCF Confidential Containers.
- [References](https://confidential-devhub.github.io/cc-book/chapters/references.md): For a comprehensive list of Confidential Computing resources, tools, papers, and projects:

## Full book (single file)

- [llms-full.txt](https://confidential-devhub.github.io/cc-book/llms-full.txt): All chapters concatenated into one document.
